sourceMovie.title: Hoodlum
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hoodlum (1997) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Bill Duke's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hoodlum clicked for you, Donnie Brasco (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hoodlum?
faq.answer: Try Donnie Brasco, Public Enemies, The Bone Collector—each shares DNA with Hoodlum while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hoodlum?
faq.answer: Hoodlum (1997) is best described as Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hoodlum worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
